message: Jake
Jennifer
There’s no response. In the room,
there’s no sign of any belongings, and of course, she’s nowhere to be seen.
Did she clear out of the room?
She said she’d let me know where she was going, but I haven’t heard from her. What’s going on?
Did she escape? If so, that means
Well,
the director, the director sure is having a tough time.
Ah,
to think Patricia got killed.
That’s just too much.
I was so scared all alone.

It’s a bland hotel room. None of the furniture or furnishings give off any sense of preference or sentiment from anyone.
The superb location almost in the heart of Manhattan is priceless. No wonder the ridiculously high accommodation fees make sense.
It seems like there’s almost no luggage brought in at all. I guess this is more of a nuisance for the intruder.
She’s facing me with a beautiful face sporting a slight smile. Is it just my misunderstanding that she looks somewhat sad?
With her disheveled hair and just a shirt thrown on, this unadorned appearance is something I never want anyone else to see.
I get the feeling she might not be feeling well today; there’s just an odd lack of energy.
I’ve decided to leave this place.
Today?
No, not today.
But this area is kind of dangerous.
I feel oddly tired.
If it's not too much trouble, I’ll contact you when I’ve decided where to go.
It's not a bother at all.
Really? That’s good.
That said, I still haven't decided where I'm going.
I'll definitely contact you when I’ve decided.
I remembered something. It’s about when Meryl came over to the director's house at Trump Tower.
Yeah.
Back then, she told Guy and Kristy that she would kill them, right? After that, when she was taken away by the guard,
she glared at Guy and the others with an incredible expression, threatening that she would definitely make them pay.
Meryl did that?
Yeah. It was scary.
Meryl didn’t actually kill Guy and the others, did she?
I don’t think so.
But she was gone for a while, right? I feel uneasy.
Meryl hasn’t done anything, right?
The director is...
It's really sad, isn't it? Things have turned out this way after they had a fight.
Yeah, for the director, that's probably something that's weighing on his mind.
Why did Patricia have to be killed?
How did it come to this?
You know the director consulted me about Patricia, right? She was against him marrying Meryl.
Was there a talk about that recently?
Hmm, it seems someone misunderstood and got angry after hearing rumors.
Poor Patricia, poor Director Fabar.
Wasn't there no head on Patricia's corpse?
That was what they said. It's terrible.
Do you think the murderer liked her face and took it for a collection?
Cindy and Patricia.
Two girls involved with the director have ended up dead. Who's next?
I understand feeling anxious, but we shouldn't think negatively.
But...
It feels like the killer might be close by, which is frightening.
It's scary.
What if it's my turn next?
I had heard about the plan to leave the hotel before, but I also don't want her to stay in this New York where such terrible events keep happening.
If she leaves New York, what will happen to my relationship with her?
I have no idea what will happen from here.
I also don't know what she's planning to do.
Meryl seems to have gone on a trip, but I haven't heard a word about where she went.
Did Meryl really kill Guy and Kristy?
No way, could Meryl have done something to Guy and the others?
I was really shocked about Patricia's death. Compared to that child's death, all the past disasters seem trivial.
My relationship with Meryl is over, and,
there's
no one left to stay by the director's side.
I don't even know what to say.
My daughter was murdered, and they still haven't found her head. There's no way to comfort him.
It's now clear that the perpetrator is intentionally targeting people in the film industry. They even took a business trip to Colorado Springs.
It's been a while since an incident happened outside New York.
But why go so far for Patricia?
Patricia was killed after Guy and the others died. This clears Guy's suspicions.
Come back again, I'll be waiting.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
